title: Chronology-aware CatBoost on raw high-cardinality IDs and crosses
summary: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics were designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage, which is especially relevant here because driver/race/compound identities are high-cardinality and rows are sequential within races.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics were designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage, which is especially relevant here because driver/race/compound identities are high-cardinality and rows are sequential within races. This directly tests whether the current manual frequency/cross features are leaving value on the table.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoost with categorical columns kept raw (`Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Year`, plus simple crosses like `Driver+Compound`, `Race+Compound`), using folds grouped by race-year and row order sorted causally within each driver-race sequence before fit. Compare against the current handcrafted feature set both with and without manual frequency features.
expected_effect: Small-to-moderate CV gain if leakage-safe category handling captures driver/race priors and interaction structure more efficiently than manual encodings.
risk: Can underperform if folds are not race-pure or if ordered statistics still absorb same-race future information through careless preprocessing outside the model.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
